description: A collection of bioactive peptides extracted from scientific literature or databases, including detailed structural, chemical, and functional annotations.

peptide_name.description: The common or systematic name of the peptide (e.g., Magainin 2, LL-37).

source_organism.description: The biological species from which the peptide was originally isolated or identified (e.g., Xenopus laevis).

peptide_sequence.description: The primary amino acid sequence using standard one-letter IUPAC codes. Example: 'GIGKFLHSAKKFGKAFVGEIMNS'.

peptide_sequence_HELM_format.description: The Hierarchical Editing Language for Macromolecules (HELM) notation, used to represent complex structures including non-natural amino acids and branching. Example: 'PEPTIDE1{G.I.G.K.F.L.H.S.A.K.K.F.G.K.A.F.V.G.E.I.M.N.S}$$$$V1.0'.

physicochemical_properties.description: Calculated or experimental physical and chemical characteristics of the peptide.

length.description: Total count of amino acid residues.

molecular_weight.description: The mass of the peptide in Daltons (Da).

net_charge.description: The total electrical charge of the peptide at physiological pH (7.4).

hydrophobicity.description: The relative hydrophobicity index (e.g., Wimley-White scale or GRAVY score).

secondary_structure.description: The predominant spatial conformation of the peptide backbone.

post_translational_modifications.description: List of covalent modifications occurring after protein biosynthesis.

modification_type.description: The specific chemical modification (e.g., C-terminal amidation, N-terminal acetylation, Disulfide bond, Phosphorylation).

position.description: The 1-based index of the modified amino acid residue within the sequence.

residue.description: The three-letter code of the amino acid being modified (e.g., 'Cys', 'Ser').

target_position.description: For cross-links like disulfide bridges, the position of the second residue involved in the bond.

biological_activities.description: Quantitative data regarding the peptide's effectiveness against specific targets.

activity_group.description: The functional category of the biological effect.

target_organism_or_cell.description: The specific strain, species, or cell line tested (e.g., E. coli ATCC 25922, HeLa cells).

activity_type.description: The metric used to quantify activity (Minimum Inhibitory Concentration, Half Maximal Inhibitory Concentration, etc.).

activity_value.description: The numerical value of the measured activity.

activity_unit.description: The unit of measurement for the activity value.

mechanism_of_action.description: Detailed description of how the peptide exerts its effect (e.g., 'Pore formation via barrel-stave model', 'Inhibition of cell wall synthesis').

toxicity_profile.description: Data regarding the safety or adverse effects of the peptide on host cells.

hemolysis.description: The ability of the peptide to rupture red blood cells.

activity_type.description: Metric for hemolytic activity (Concentration causing 50% hemolysis or percentage at a specific dose).

cytotoxicity.description: Toxic effects on nucleated mammalian cells.

cell_line.description: The specific mammalian cell line used for testing (e.g., HEK293, Vero).
